stated_correlations.description: Authors state that kinetic inductance scales inversely with NbSe2 layer thickness (1/d dependence) across all 12 devices
stated_correlations.value: Film thickness d (nm) vs Sheet kinetic inductance Lk,sq (nH/sq)
stated_correlations.notes: Negative correlation following approximate 1/d power law; Lk,sq ranges from 1.195 nH/sq at ~2 nm to 0.004 nH/sq at 52 nm

stated_correlations.description: Authors state that the ratio Lk,sq/Lk,sq,clean increases monotonically from 0.7 to 15 as sample thickness decreases toward monolayer, indicating increasing dirty-limit contribution in thinner samples
stated_correlations.value: Film thickness (layer number) vs Lk,sq/Lk,sq,clean ratio
stated_correlations.notes: Negative correlation; thin samples show dirty-limit dominant behavior, thick samples show clean-limit behavior

stated_correlations.description: Authors state that increasing Au encapsulation thickness leads to a decrease in the effective superconducting gap (minigap) of the resonator
stated_correlations.value: Au encapsulation thickness (nm) vs Effective superconducting gap / Δ0
stated_correlations.notes: Negative correlation; gap decreases monotonically from ~1.0 at 5 nm to near 0 at 323 nm, fitted by Usadel proximity effect model

evidence_records.deposition_method: ALD (atomic layer deposition) using TBTDEN precursor and H2/N2 plasma
ALD (atomic layer deposition) with local ALE thinning for weak link
DC magnetron sputtering (UHV) for Ta; electron-beam evaporation for Al/AlOx junction (HV or UHV depending on qubit)

stated_correlations.description: Authors state Tc decreases slightly as deposition temperature increases for 1 ks films
stated_correlations.notes: Negative correlation for 1 ks films: higher deposition temperature associated with slightly lower Tc

evidence_records.deposition_method: ALD (atomic layer deposition) using TBTDEN precursor and H2/N2 plasma
ALD (atomic layer deposition) with local ALE thinning for weak link

stated_correlations.description: Authors state Qi does not degrade with increased kinetic inductance (i.e. proximity to SIT) in ALE-treated films
stated_correlations.value: Normal-state sheet resistance RN (proxy for proximity to SIT) vs Qi at single-photon power
stated_correlations.notes: No correlation observed: Qi remains ~10^6 independent of RN in the range investigated
stated_correlations.term_a: Normal-state sheet resistance RN (proxy for proximity to SIT)

stated_correlations.description: Authors use the difference in loss tangent between this sample (90-day saturation) and Row 5 (2-hour exposure) to isolate the intrinsic AlOx loss tangent, attributing the difference entirely to the 1.2 nm oxide thickness change
stated_correlations.value: oxide thickness change (1.2 nm) vs surface loss tangent change (0.65e-3)
stated_correlations.notes: Positive correlation used to derive tan_delta_AlOx = (1.74 ± 0.7) × 10^-2

mapping_notes: 'Internal quality factor Qi (exceeds 10^6 for ALE-treated films vs <10^6 for untreated)' → Qi_internal: ambiguous

stated_correlations.description: Authors state ALE treatment improves surface quality, which correlates with higher measured Qi in CPW resonators
stated_correlations.value: ALE surface treatment (roughness reduction >50%) vs Internal quality factor Qi (exceeds 10^6 for ALE-treated films vs <10^6 for untreated)
stated_correlations.notes: Positive correlation: ALE-treated films with lower roughness show higher Qi
stated_correlations.term_b: Internal quality factor Qi (exceeds 10^6 for ALE-treated films vs <10^6 for untreated)

stated_correlations.description: Authors state that the self-Kerr coefficient magnitude increases with decreasing film thickness (larger Kerr nonlinearity for thinner, higher-Lk samples)
stated_correlations.value: Sheet kinetic inductance Lk,sq vs Self-Kerr coefficient K/2pi magnitude
stated_correlations.notes: Positive correlation; Kerr ranges from -0.006 Hz/photon (bulk) to -14.7 Hz/photon (monolayer)

stated_correlations.description: Authors state that modulation depth δ increases with coherence length ξ in Ginzburg-Landau simulations of SSQ-50
stated_correlations.value: coherence length ξ vs SQUID modulation depth δ
stated_correlations.notes: positive correlation: δ increases from 25% to 40% as ξ increases from 60 to 140 nm

mapping_notes: 'Vortex activation temperature Tact / microwave loss' → json:vortex_activation_temperature_K: ambiguous

stated_correlations.description: Authors state that clean-limit films (type A, higher RRR, larger l) show lower vortex activation temperatures and more anomalous microwave loss compared to dirty-limit films (type B, lower RRR, smaller l)
stated_correlations.value: RRR / mean free path / superconducting limit vs Vortex activation temperature Tact / microwave loss
stated_correlations.notes: Negative correlation: higher RRR and longer l (clean limit) correlates with lower Tact and greater vortex-motion-induced microwave loss
stated_correlations.term_a: RRR / mean free path / superconducting limit
stated_correlations.term_b: Vortex activation temperature Tact / microwave loss

stated_correlations.description: Authors state that as SPR decreases, QTLS,0 increases consistently without saturation for Ta-on-Si resonators, in contrast to Ta-on-sapphire where QTLS,0 saturates at large device sizes due to bulk substrate loss
stated_correlations.value: Metal-substrate surface participation ratio (pMS) vs QTLS,0 (TLS-limited quality factor)
stated_correlations.notes: Negative correlation (lower SPR → higher QTLS,0), with no bulk-loss saturation for Si substrate

stated_correlations.description: Plasma oxidation at room temperature shows insensitivity to time: oxide thickness within 0.3 nm for 10 vs 30 min exposures
stated_correlations.value: Oxidation time (10 vs 30 min) vs TaOx thickness (7.64 vs 7.33 nm)
stated_correlations.notes: No significant correlation — time-insensitive within tested range at room temperature

stated_correlations.description: Authors state that switching from HV to UHV junction deposition significantly improves T2E (statistically significant at P < 0.01 by Mann-Whitney U test) without significantly changing T1 or Qavg
stated_correlations.value: Junction deposition vacuum (HV vs UHV) vs T2E
stated_correlations.notes: Positive correlation: UHV deposition yields higher T2E (mean 1.2T1 vs 0.45T1 for HV)

stated_correlations.description: Authors state films with higher RRR values tend to adopt (222) orientation while films with smaller RRR display mixed (110)+(222) or dominant (110) orientation
stated_correlations.notes: Positive correlation: dominant (222) orientation associated with higher RRR; dominant (110) associated with lower RRR
